body_txt: An Orphaned Reference is a scene or line that refers to something that has been cut from the final version. In milder cases, this only means that what was supposed to be a Meaningful Echo loses its additional meaning; in more severe cases, the lost background information can cause apparent Noodle Incidents , Plot Holes or Ass Pulls . In other cases it may result in the Kuleshov Effect , where without supplemental context the material takes on an entirely different meaning. Compare The Artifact , The Other Marty . See also Dub-Induced Plot Hole , Adaptation-Induced Plot Hole , Adaptation Explanation Extrication , Lost in Translation ; all adaptation and dub examples go there. When the reference is found in licensed material, it's Early Draft Tie-In . When it's referring to components of a joke In-Universe , they may be an Orphaned Setup and/or an Orphaned Punchline . Some video game examples may overlap with Dummied Out and Missing Secret . Development Gag is when this is done deliberately as a meta joke . Not to be confused with Orphaned Etymology , where the reference in question isn't referring to something cut from the final version, but instead is referring to a person, location, or concept that doesn't exist in the setting.
